name: Access Control List System
description: Implement a role-based access control system across two modules. Module acl_types.py must define: Permission dataclass with fields resource (str), action (str); Role dataclass with fields name (str), permissions (set of (resource, action) tuples), inherits (list[str], parent role names); AccessDecision enum ALLOW, DENY, NOT_APPLICABLE. Module acl_engine.py must import from acl_types.py and implement Engine class with methods: define_role(role: Role); assign_role(user: str, role_name: str); revoke_role(user: str, role_name: str) -> bool; grant(role_name: str, resource: str, action: str); deny(role_name: str, resource: str, action: str) explicit deny that overrides grants; check(user: str, resource: str, action: str) -> AccessDecision; effective_permissions(user: str) -> set[tuple[str, str]] computed across all assigned roles and their inheritance. Inheritance: child role gets parent's permissions; explicit DENY beats inherited ALLOW.
test_imports: from acl_types import Permission, Role, AccessDecision
signature:
# acl_types.py
from dataclasses import dataclass, field
from enum import Enum

@dataclass
class Permission:
    resource: str
    action: str

@dataclass
class Role:
    name: str
    permissions: set = field(default_factory=set)
    inherits: list = field(default_factory=list)

class AccessDecision(Enum):
    ALLOW = 'allow'
    DENY = 'deny'
    NOT_APPLICABLE = 'not_applicable'

# acl_engine.py
from acl_types import Permission, Role, AccessDecision

class Engine:
    def __init__(self): ...
    def define_role(self, role: Role) -> None: ...
    def assign_role(self, user: str, role_name: str) -> None: ...
    def revoke_role(self, user: str, role_name: str) -> bool: ...
    def grant(self, role_name: str, resource: str, action: str) -> None: ...
    def deny(self, role_name: str, resource: str, action: str) -> None: ...
    def check(self, user: str, resource: str, action: str) -> AccessDecision: ...
    def effective_permissions(self, user: str) -> set: ...
tests:
def test_grant_allows():
    e = Engine()
    e.define_role(Role('reader'))
    e.grant('reader', 'doc', 'read')
    e.assign_role('alice', 'reader')
    assert e.check('alice', 'doc', 'read') == AccessDecision.ALLOW

def test_no_permission_not_applicable():
    e = Engine()
    e.define_role(Role('reader'))
    e.grant('reader', 'doc', 'read')
    e.assign_role('alice', 'reader')
    assert e.check('alice', 'doc', 'write') == AccessDecision.NOT_APPLICABLE

def test_deny_blocks():
    e = Engine()
    e.define_role(Role('user'))
    e.grant('user', 'doc', 'read')
    e.deny('user', 'doc', 'read')
    e.assign_role('alice', 'user')
    assert e.check('alice', 'doc', 'read') == AccessDecision.DENY

def test_inheritance():
    e = Engine()
    e.define_role(Role('reader'))
    e.grant('reader', 'doc', 'read')
    e.define_role(Role('editor', inherits=['reader']))
    e.grant('editor', 'doc', 'write')
    e.assign_role('alice', 'editor')
    assert e.check('alice', 'doc', 'read') == AccessDecision.ALLOW
    assert e.check('alice', 'doc', 'write') == AccessDecision.ALLOW

def test_deny_overrides_inherit():
    e = Engine()
    e.define_role(Role('admin'))
    e.grant('admin', 'sys', 'delete')
    e.define_role(Role('limited', inherits=['admin']))
    e.deny('limited', 'sys', 'delete')
    e.assign_role('bob', 'limited')
    assert e.check('bob', 'sys', 'delete') == AccessDecision.DENY

def test_multiple_roles():
    e = Engine()
    e.define_role(Role('a'))
    e.grant('a', 'r1', 'read')
    e.define_role(Role('b'))
    e.grant('b', 'r2', 'write')
    e.assign_role('user', 'a')
    e.assign_role('user', 'b')
    assert e.check('user', 'r1', 'read') == AccessDecision.ALLOW
    assert e.check('user', 'r2', 'write') == AccessDecision.ALLOW

def test_revoke():
    e = Engine()
    e.define_role(Role('reader'))
    e.grant('reader', 'doc', 'read')
    e.assign_role('alice', 'reader')
    assert e.revoke_role('alice', 'reader') is True
    assert e.check('alice', 'doc', 'read') == AccessDecision.NOT_APPLICABLE

def test_revoke_unassigned():
    e = Engine()
    e.define_role(Role('x'))
    assert e.revoke_role('alice', 'x') is False

def test_effective_permissions():
    e = Engine()
    e.define_role(Role('reader'))
    e.grant('reader', 'doc', 'read')
    e.define_role(Role('editor', inherits=['reader']))
    e.grant('editor', 'doc', 'write')
    e.assign_role('alice', 'editor')
    perms = e.effective_permissions('alice')
    assert ('doc', 'read') in perms
    assert ('doc', 'write') in perms

def test_chained_inheritance():
    e = Engine()
    e.define_role(Role('a'))
    e.grant('a', 'r', 'x')
    e.define_role(Role('b', inherits=['a']))
    e.define_role(Role('c', inherits=['b']))
    e.assign_role('u', 'c')
    assert e.check('u', 'r', 'x') == AccessDecision.ALLOW
constraints: Roles must be defined before being assigned
Explicit DENY at any level beats ALLOW
If neither grant nor deny applies, return NOT_APPLICABLE
User can have multiple roles; their permissions union with deny overrides
